reasoning: Peer set (Denzi helmets) is dominated by cool silver-gray metallic tones with dark neutral shadows and controlled specular highlights. Candidate relic is a warm amber-gold medallion with a saturated teal/blue gem center — the warmth delta is large and the teal hue has essentially zero representation in the peer palette. While a golden relic is naturally warm (item-type excuse accepted), the DEGREE of saturation on both the gold ring and the gem pushes outside the peer family's restrained color temperature. Outline weight and outline color (dark/near-black) are consistent — PASS. Shadow direction is readable top-left consistent with peers — PASS. Shadow count appears single-layer; peers use 1-2 layers — borderline PASS. Highlight intensity: the gem center's specular pop is brighter and more 'glassy' than anything in the peer set, which keeps highlights muted/metallic — mild FAIL. Color temperature: warm amber vs cool silver — marginal FAIL. Detail density: candidate has fewer distinct pixel clusters than the intricate helmet engravings — mild FAIL. Proportion: circular medallion, appropriate for relic type — PASS. Rendering style: pixel art at correct resolution with clear outlines — PASS. Palette overlap: only dark outlines and a sliver of gold trim shared; teal gem and saturated orange-gold are orphan hues — FAIL. Charm level: gem glow gives a magical toy quality slightly above the stoic-RPG tone of the peer helmets — minor mismatch. Net: style bones are right (pixel art, outlines, shadow direction) but palette temperature and gem saturation break family cohesion enough to land in marginal territory.
